channel.title: Configuration on YoSQL - write more SQL
channel.description: Recent content in Configuration on YoSQL - write more SQL

item.title: annotateClasses
item.description: Controls whether @Generated annotations be added to generated classes. Defaults to false. Configuration Options Option: ‘false’ The default value of the annotateClasses configuration option is false. Setting the option to false therefore produces the same code generated as the default configuration. package com.example.persistence; public class SomeRepository { // ... rest of generated code } Option: ‘true’ Changing the annotateClasses configuration option to true adds the @Generated annotation to every generated class.

item.title: Annotations
item.description: The annotations configuration can be used to control how YoSQL outputs annotations. Tooling Maven In order to use YoSQL together with Maven, take a look at the tooling documentation for Maven. <build> <plugins> ... <plugin> <groupId>wtf.metio.yosql</groupId> <artifactId>yosql-maven-plugin</artifactId> <configuration> <annotations> <configOption>configValue</configOption> </annotations> </configuration> </plugin> ... </plugins> </build> Gradle In order to use YoSQL together with Gradle, take a look at the tooling documentation for Gradle. plugins { id("wtf.metio.yosql") } yosql { annotations { configOption = configValue } } Bazel In order to use YoSQL together with Bazel, take a look at the tooling documentation for Bazel.

item.title: basePackageName
item.description: The base package name for the generated code. Defaults to com.example.persistence. Configuration Options Option: ‘com.example.persistence’ The default value of the basePackageName configuration option is com.example.persistence. Setting the option to com.example.persistence therefore produces the same code generated as the default configuration without any configuration option set. It produces code similar to this: package com.example.persistence; public class SomeRepository { // ... rest of generated code } Option: ‘your.own.domain’ Changing the basePackageName configuration option to your.

item.title: Repositories
item.description: The repositories configuration can be used to control how YoSQL outputs repositories. Tooling Maven In order to use YoSQL together with Maven, take a look at the tooling documentation for Maven. <build> <plugins> ... <plugin> <groupId>wtf.metio.yosql</groupId> <artifactId>yosql-maven-plugin</artifactId> <configuration> <repositories> <configOption>configValue</configOption> </repositories> </configuration> </plugin> ... </plugins> </build> Gradle In order to use YoSQL together with Gradle, take a look at the tooling documentation for Gradle. plugins { id("wtf.metio.yosql") } yosql { repositories { configOption = configValue } } Bazel In order to use YoSQL together with Bazel, take a look at the tooling documentation for Bazel.
